old_exact:
Ibrahim Hassan Nur appeals from the district court's order denying his

[¶1] application
new_exact:
[¶1]   Ibrahim Hassan Nur appeals from the district court's order denying his
application
source_quote:
[¶1]   Ibrahim Hassan Nur appeals from the district court's order denying his
application for post-conviction relief. Nur argues he did not voluntarily, knowingly,
and intelligently waive his right to counsel, and he argues he received ineffective
assistance of counsel. The district court did not err in summarily denying Nur's
application for post-conviction relief for misuse of process on the issue of his waiver
to the right to counsel. Also, the district court did not err in denying Nur's application
for post-conviction relief based on ineffective assistance of counsel and its findings
of fact were not clearly erroneous. We affirm under N.D.R.App.P. 35.1(a)(2) and (6).
evidence: The DB misplaces the [¶1] paragraph marker. In the DB, the marker appears after 'his' with a blank line separating it from the preceding text. In the authoritative PDF source, [¶1] appears at the start of the sentence introducing the opinion body, immediately before 'Ibrahim Hassan Nur'. This is a paragraph sequencing error where the paragraph marker got separated from its intended paragraph.

evidence: The authoritative PDF contains a section marker 'V' (centered on its own line, line 351) positioned between the end of [¶26] and [¶27] 'We affirm the judgments.' The database text incorrectly places this 'V' marker after [¶28] (the justices' signatures) instead of before [¶27], which disrupts the proper document structure established throughout the opinion where section markers (I, II, III, IV) appear before their respective content paragraphs.

[¶12] We conclude Horvath has raised reasonable inferences creating a genuine issue of material fact regarding his counsel's trial performance with respect to the use of exhibit 24. We therefore conclude Horvath is entitled to an evidentiary hearing on this claim.

                                          III
[¶13] We reverse the district court order summarily dismissing Horvath's petition for post-conviction relief and remand to the district court for an evidentiary hearing.
evidence: The section heading 'III' marks the start of section III. In the source PDF (lines 157-159), 'III' appears on line 157 before [¶13] on line 158. In the DB, the paragraph sequence is reversed: [¶13] appears first (line 67), followed by blank lines and then 'III' (line 69). The correct structural order per the source is to place the section heading III before the paragraph [¶13].
